body.title: Raise consult confidence threshold 0.3 → 0.55
body.result: Superseded by T-consult-confidence-gate (commit b5469f55) — ticket described 'raise 0.3 floor to 0.55' but no 0.3 constant existed; Akien clarified scope to 'add abort-on-low-confidence gate', shipped under T-consult-confidence-gate.
body.description:
Consult observe-and-tune Finding 1: nothing in 104 sessions fell under confidence 0.62. The 0.3 floor is vestigial — sub-threshold confabulations can't currently surface as 'consult aborted, escalate to tier.4'. Raise to 0.55 so edge cases get explicit escalation instead of silent low-confidence accept.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/consult.py (constants at top), tests/test_consult_primitive.py (update boundary tests).
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — one-line threshold constant change + test update. OUT — dynamic thresholding, per-problem-kind thresholds.
**Test plan:** existing consult boundary tests updated to 0.55; 28 T-consult-primitive tests green.
